questions.text: Who is the head of the government of India?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister is the head of the government.

questions.text: In which year did India become a Republic?
questions.explanation: India became a Republic on 26 January 1950.

questions.text: The national song of India is ____.
questions.explanation: 'Vande Mataram' is the national song.

questions.text: The Parliament of India is in New Delhi.
questions.explanation: The Parliament is in New Delhi. True.

questions.text: Which is the largest country in the world by area?
questions.explanation: Russia is the largest country by area.

questions.text: Which is the smallest continent?
questions.explanation: Australia is the smallest continent.

questions.text: The Great Wall is located in the country of ____.
questions.explanation: The Great Wall of China is in China.

questions.text: The Pyramids of Giza are in Egypt.
questions.explanation: The Pyramids are in Egypt. True.

questions.text: Which planet is known as the 'Blue Planet'?
questions.explanation: Earth is called the Blue Planet because of its water.

questions.text: Which is the smallest planet in our solar system?
questions.explanation: Mercury is the smallest planet.

questions.text: The speed of light is faster than the speed of ____.
questions.explanation: Light travels much faster than sound.

questions.text: Diamond is the hardest natural substance.
questions.explanation: Diamond is the hardest natural material. True.

questions.text: Who discovered gravity by watching an apple fall?
questions.explanation: Sir Isaac Newton is linked with discovering gravity.

questions.text: ____ was the first Indian woman to go into space.
questions.explanation: Kalpana Chawla was the first Indian woman in space.

questions.text: A.P.J. Abdul Kalam served as the President of India.
questions.explanation: Dr. Kalam was the 11th President of India. True.

questions.text: Which is the longest river in the world?
questions.explanation: The Nile is generally considered the longest river.

questions.text: The currency of Japan is the ____.
questions.explanation: Japan's currency is the Yen.

questions.text: Mount Everest lies in the Himalayas.
questions.explanation: Mount Everest is in the Himalayas. True.

questions.text: Which is the national fruit of India?
questions.explanation: Mango is the national fruit of India.

questions.text: The 'City of Lakes' in India is ____.
questions.explanation: Udaipur is called the City of Lakes.

questions.text: The Sundarbans is famous for the Royal Bengal Tiger.
questions.explanation: The Sundarbans is home to the Royal Bengal Tiger. True.

questions.text: Which organ is the largest in the human body?
questions.explanation: The skin is the largest organ of the human body.

questions.text: The gas we use for breathing is ____.
questions.explanation: We breathe in oxygen.

questions.text: Bats use sound to find their way in the dark.
questions.explanation: Bats use echolocation (sound) to navigate. True.

questions.text: Where were the first modern Olympic Games held in 1896?
questions.explanation: The first modern Olympics were held in Athens, Greece.

questions.text: In cricket, scoring 100 runs by a batsman is called a ____.
questions.explanation: 100 runs is called a century.

questions.text: Saina Nehwal is a famous Indian badminton player.
questions.explanation: Saina Nehwal is a top Indian badminton player. True.

questions.text: Which Indian space agency launched Chandrayaan?
questions.explanation: ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation) launched Chandrayaan.

questions.text: The Statue of Liberty is in the country ____.
questions.explanation: The Statue of Liberty is in the USA (New York).

questions.text: We should keep our surroundings clean to prevent diseases.
questions.explanation: Cleanliness prevents diseases. True.
